description: Detective fiction requires specific element configurations: an investigator, a solvable puzzle with fair clueing, and a revelation structure that rewards attentive reading.

character_constraints.description: A detective or investigator whose analytical skill drives the story's progression.
The perpetrator — must be a character who can be logically identified from available evidence.
A partner, assistant, or informant who aids the investigation.
At least one suspect whose true nature is concealed until the revelation.
A witness or informant who provides partial testimony that must be assembled.

relationship_constraints.description: The detective and perpetrator must be in intellectual opposition.

place_constraints.description: The investigation territory — crime scenes, interrogation rooms, archives.
The world before the crime that establishes the normal order disrupted.

object_constraints.description: The central puzzle — a crime, disappearance, or anomaly that must be solved.
Physical evidence or testimony that, when properly assembled, reveals the truth.
The breakthrough clue — the final piece that unlocks the solution.

element_rules.description: Fair clueing: all evidence needed to solve the mystery must be presented to the audience before the reveal.
element_rules.testable_condition: All 'document' objects appear in timeline moments before the 'reveals' transition that solves the mystery.

element_rules.description: The detective must solve the case through reasoning, not coincidence or supernatural means.
element_rules.testable_condition: The protagonist's 'learns' transitions are preceded by investigation actions, not lucky breaks.

element_rules.description: At least one false lead must misdirect before the true solution emerges.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'shapeshifter' character appears as a suspect in a timeline moment before being cleared.

element_rules.description: The perpetrator must be a character who has appeared in the story, not someone introduced at the reveal.
element_rules.testable_condition: The antagonist appears as a participant in at least one timeline moment before the final revelation.
